# Everett Labs

> Independent software lab for useful tools, open systems, and edge-native experiments.

Everett Labs is built by Frank. The site documents practical open-source software for the web,
browsers, WordPress, and Linux. Public pages are written in English and link to the source repository
for every listed project.

## Core pages

- [Home](https://everettlabs.dev/): Studio overview, featured projects, engineering principles, and writing links.
- [Projects](https://everettlabs.dev/projects): Curated index of all approved Everett Labs projects.
- [About](https://everettlabs.dev/about): Studio identity, principles, ownership, and contact information.
- [Coffee](https://everettlabs.dev/coffee): Official support options for open-source work.

## Projects

- [Linketry](https://everettlabs.dev/projects/linketry): Self-hosted link management, analytics, and monitoring — built for stable redirects, data ownership, and long-term operation.
- [GloboKit](https://everettlabs.dev/projects/globokit): A practical browser-based toolkit for international operations, logistics, calculations, barcodes, and everyday productivity.
- [FavGrove](https://everettlabs.dev/projects/favgrove): A local-first bookmark workspace for Chrome and Edge, designed for large collections, fast search, link scanning, and safe cleanup.
- [Citeoryx](https://everettlabs.dev/projects/citeoryx): A WordPress content health and AI discoverability engine for inventory, issue prioritization, optimization, and lifecycle monitoring.
- [LinkVitals](https://everettlabs.dev/projects/linkvitals): A privacy-friendly WordPress link health and SEO auditor for broken links, redirects, orphaned content, and external-link risks.
- [VPS Scripts](https://everettlabs.dev/projects/vps-scripts): A modular Linux operations toolkit for system maintenance, diagnostics, benchmarking, environment setup, and server administration.
- [Nezha Cleaner](https://everettlabs.dev/projects/nezha-cleaner): A safety-focused removal utility for Nezha Agent and Dashboard installations across native and Docker environments.
- [DistroLift](https://everettlabs.dev/projects/distrolift): A cautious, step-by-step upgrade tool for Debian and Ubuntu LTS servers.
- [NodeLoc Bench](https://everettlabs.dev/projects/nodeloc-bench): A community-maintained server benchmarking launcher that combines performance, IP, connectivity, routing, and network diagnostic tests.

## External sources

- [GitHub](https://github.com/everett7623): Source repositories and release history.
- [Seedloc](https://seedloc.com): Technical notes, deployment guides, and experiments.

The project pages and linked repositories are the authoritative sources for current capabilities,
status, licensing, safety notices, and upstream attribution.
